observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE direct:

a. The Municipal Accountant to:

i. Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reverse the recording of PPEs in the book of accounts;
ii. Prepare necessary adjusting entries to record the transfer of the cost of completed projects booked as PPE under the Trust Fund to the General Fund; and
iii. Provide subsidiary ledgers for the CIP Account.

b. The Inventory Committee/Property Custodian to prepare and submit the IIRUP and other required documents in accordance with the Manual on PPE Appraisal and Disposal to facilitate the disposal of all unserviceable properties at the earliest possible time, pursuant to Section 79 of PD No. 1445, Sections 379-381 of RA 7160, and in accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular No. 89-296.

We recommended and the that Management agreed that the LCE the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments so as to arrive at the correct balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements. Further, ensure that transactions substantiated with necessary supporting documents will be recorded as valid obligation of the municipality.

We recommended that and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive to require the:

a. Municipal Accountant to:

i. Set-up the RPT and SET Receivable for CY 2022;
ii. Analyze and record the proposed adjusting/correcting entries in the books to rectify the noted overstatement/understatement of the RPT and SET Receivables and its related accounts; and
iii. Follow the correct accounting entries regarding advance payments of real property and special education taxes in order to reflect the correct amount of affected accounts in the financial statements.

b. Assistant Municipal Treasurer to:

i. Furnish the Municipal account of the Certified List of Taxpayer with corresponding Taxes Payable for CY 2022 to be served as the basis in setting-up the RPT and SET Receivable for CY 2022; and
ii. Furnish the Municipal Accountant the Report on Distribution of Real Property Tax Collection every month for proper recording of the RPT collections.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Local Chie Executive require:

a. Municipal Health Office to furnish the Accounting Office with a detailed list of the equipment and supplies received from donation and other pertinent documents to support the appropriate recording by the Municipal Accountant; and

b. Municipal Accountant to:

i. Recognize in the books the donations received with costs (if determined) in accordance with existing regulations; and
ii. Recognize the donations at their fair value as at the date of receipt, which may be ascertained by reference to an active market, or by appraisal for donations received without costs as provided in Paragraph 97 of the IPSAS No. 23.

We recommended and the management agreed that Local Chief Executive direct the:

a. Municipal Health Officer to:

i. Assign the receipt, segregation, safekeeping and transfer to PHO of the used and discarded vials functions to Sanitation Inspector (SI).
ii. Require SI to properly store and transfer the vials together with the consolidated Form A – Reverse Logistic to PHO every Friday of the vaccination week;\niii. Record all donations-in-kind received by the RHU in the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, duly supported with the (1) Acknowledgment Receipts, (2) proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries not necessarily in the required form, and (3) the inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any; and
iv. Submit a copy of the same to the Storekeeper and the Municipal Accountant.

b. Storekeeper to:

i. Assist the MHO in the inspection and acceptance of all donations-in-kind, and in the conduct of inventory of the remaining undistributed items, if any; and
ii. Maintain copies of the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and its supporting documents; and
iii. Prepare and maintain a Registry of Donated COVID-19 Vaccines

c. Municipal Accountant to make a disclosure in the Notes to FS, based on the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM, as to the quantity of received, issued and balance of all donation-in-kind received by the Municipality, with categorization as to food items, medicines and medical supplies, hygiene kits, and others.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the:

a. The MDRRM Council to properly plan the utilization of the unexpended balance from the prior years’ QRF and Mitigation Fund-MOOE.

b. LDRRM focal person and responsible personnel to:

i. prepare the subsequent years’ annual LDRRMFIP in accordance with the COA Circular No. 2012-002 and incorporate the PPAs to be funded from the unexpended fund;
ii. submit monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF; and
iii. strictly follow the guidelines on the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund as provided in Section 21 of RA No. 10121.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE:

a. Instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the offices concerned to:

i. Determine and address accordingly the causes of the delay and non-implementation of the projects; and
ii. Facilitate the immediate implementation of the same.

b. Direct the Municipal Engineer and the BAC to periodically provide the MBO and the Municipal Accountant with the status of projects and the procurements undertaken by the Municipality to assist the concerned offices in the periodic review and monitoring of the balances of current and continuing appropriations and inform immediately the MBO and Municipal Accountant whenever there are changes on the awarded PPAs and problems in the project’s implementation to make the necessary adjustment in the financial reports relative to the affected PPAs.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Human Resource Management Officer to:

a. Submit a duly executed Individual Job Order Contract or Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) containing a well-defined job description stating therein the specific work to be assigned to each JO personnel; and

b. Submit a copy of Detailed Individual Accomplishment Reports to facilitate comparison with the job description stated on their contracts, thereby, ensuring the protection of government funds from unnecessary expenditures.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to compute, withhold and remit the respective income taxes during the year and onwards as provided under the RR No. 11-2018 dated January 31, 2018, in compliance with the prevailing tax laws and regulations.

The RSMI should be prepared on a monthly basis to cover the issuance of inventory items for the month to avoid accumulation of unrecorded issuance. (8) The Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to drop all the materials and supplies already issued to end-user from the books for recording purposes.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management formulate an action plan to implement the recommendations, immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI form and submit the accomplished AAPSI form to the Audit Team, copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee on Appropriations and Senate Committee on Finance pursuant to Section 93 of R.A. 10651.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Finding No. 10

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management undertake the following measures, to wit: a. Strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 14 of Executive Order No. 298 and Sections 4.1.2 and 4.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002; b. Direct the accountant to enforce the liquidations of cash advances through the issuance of demand letters to concerned officials and employees; c. Direct the Accountant to restore back the amount of P1,764,887.72 liquidations without supporting documents in CY 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Finding No. 6

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the designated Municipal Accountant be required to
prepare and submit the Statement of Financial Performance for the year ended
December 31, 2016 under Trust Fund, Statement of Comparison of Budget and
Actual Amounts under the General Fund, and to disclose the details of some line
items in the Statement of Cash Flows in the Notes to Combined Financial
Statements and prepare the required Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from
Operating Activities to Surplus/Deficit as required under Philippine Public
Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) No. 1.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant-Designate draw a journal
entry voucher to record the donated equipment to its proper asset accounts and
provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements. Moreover,
require the Property and Supply unit to record the in-kind donations other than
PPE in the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM, and prepare monthly
report on the receipt, distribution/utilization and or issuance of in-kind
donations pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We recommend that Management maximize utilization of the mitigation
fund in implementing the various programs for risk reduction and management
in order to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant
be required to submit the disbursement vouchers to support the recorded check
disbursements totaling P7,897,138.39and the204 pcs. of cancelled checks.
Henceforth, strictly observe the timely submission of the accounts in accordance
with Section 347 of republic Act 7160, Section105 of PD1445, and Section 7.2.1
(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. This
recommendation is without prejudice to the result of post audit of the DVs once
submitted.

We recommend that management limit the use of 20% Development
Funds for purposes that are outlined in DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommend that the Local School Board prepare an Annual School
Board Budget to be approved by all members of the Local School Board as
required in Section 99 of RA 7160 and duly supported with programs, projects
and activities that are aligned with Section 100 of RA 7160. Likewise, see to it
that all claims against government funds shall be supported with complete
documentations.

We recommend that a full-blown information and dissemination
campaign on proper solid waste management be conducted so that the
constituents will be fully aware of the benefits derived from the program.

We recommend that management submit the list and details of all on-
going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be
implemented during the year pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated
January 30, 2013.

We recommend that management strictly observe the guidelines set forth
by PhilHealth Circular No. 010, s. 2012 particularly on the granting of
Honoraria. We also recommend the return of the collected honoraria of the
local government officials and employees who were not entitled to receive such
honoraria.

We recommend that the management submit immediately the LDRRMF Investment Plan and Report on Sources and Utilization of Fund as required in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. Likewise, submit a GAD Plan and Budget as well as GAD accomplishment reports as required in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Finding No. 7

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly observe the guidelines on the appropriation of the 20% Development Fund and to implement the programs, projects and activities as planned.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Finding No. 11
observations.reason: See related discussion on Finding No. 5, CY 2013 Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management procure common use supplies and equipment only at the DBM, PS Depot as mandated by AO No. 17 dated July 28, 2011 and DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011. Common–use supplies and equipment not available at the PS should be procured in accordance with IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Finding No. 5
observations.reason: Unavailability of some supplies in the PS Depot.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned officers and employees be required to submit: (i) duly approved and properly accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets which should be serially numbered, and (ii) Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption of government motor transportation and equipment. Henceforth, strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 in order to control and regulate the use of government vehicles.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Finding No. 6
observations.reason: Management has yet to request the copy of vouchers for compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat/Municipal Accountant to prepare an Annual Procurement Plan so that only goods and services that are crucial to the efficient discharge of government operation are procured.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Finding No. 8
observations.reason: Some department heads failed to submit their procurement plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant be directed to strictly observe the timely submission of accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Finding No. 10
observations.reason: Still delayed in submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the disbursing officer liquidate the cash advances or submit the required documents for audit, or refund the unexpended balances of the cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Finding No. 2
observations.reason: Some Cash Advances of Municipal Treasurer were not yet liquidated as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly comply with the provisions of Section 74 of PD 1445 and Sections 3.2 & 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-001 dated October 2, 1996. We also recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books to arrive at correct records and balances.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Finding No. 3
observations.reason: TF & GF had no separate 2012 reconciliation per bank account and adjusting entries were not shown. GF had only one month reconciliation (Dec.2016 only).

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management observe the guidelines set forth in RA 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations in all their procurement activities. We also recommend management use the appropriate accounts in recording transactions for a clear and fair presentation.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Finding No. 4
observations.reason: Municipal Bldg was also made thru cash advance. Management will adhere to procurement rules. Promised compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the liquidation reports/documents for audit, if any, or effect the necessary adjustments in the books to record back the amount adjusted to come up with a correct account balance.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Finding No. 5
observations.reason: Still several cash advances were not yet liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal Mayor undertakes the following measures, to wit: 1. Direct the Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices to properly maintain property records and to conduct regular reconciliation of both records so that errors could be detected and necessary adjustments be made to come up with correct balances. 2. Create a committee to conduct an inventory of all properties of the municipal government in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System for local government units, Volume I. 3. Send concerned officials and employees to attend trainings on Property Management.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Finding No. 7
observations.reason: Will create a committee to undertake the thorough inventory of all equipment and properties of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned employees liquidate immediately the above cash advances and for the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the above vouchers and supporting documents. Henceforth, adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of Sec. 100 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 6.04 and 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006.
observations.managementAction: AAR, Finding No. 10
observations.reason: Directed all concerned that all the Trust Fund vouchers be submitted as soon as possible. Some vouchers submitted; several cash advances remained unliquidated as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipality observe the guidelines set forth in RA 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations and LOI 755 in all their procurement activities.
